title: Trump Signs 25 Executive Orders in 2026, Surpassing Full First-Term Total
detail: As of March 25, 2026, President Trump has signed 25 executive orders in 2026 alone (EO 14372 through EO 14396), and 250 total in his second term — surpassing the 220 he signed across the entirety of his first term and the 162 signed by President Biden. The pace reflects the administration's aggressive use of executive authority across domains including trade, immigration, AI, housing, and cultural policy. Key March actions included housing deregulation, the 'Made in America' enforcement order, the AI framework, and a new anti-fraud task force.

title: State Department Expands Visa Bond Policy to 18 Countries Effective April 2
detail: The State Department announced in mid-March 2026 that its visa bond policy would be extended to 12 additional countries effective April 2, 2026, bringing the total to 18 affected countries. Newly covered countries include Cambodia, Ethiopia, Georgia, Grenada, Lesotho, Mauritius, Mongolia, Mozambique, Nicaragua, Papua New Guinea, Seychelles, and Tunisia. The visa bond requirement compels nationals from designated countries to post financial bonds before receiving US visas, part of the administration's broader immigration enforcement tightening.
